flags.concern: timeline-waveform-eligibility: the plan's default 'show waveform on muted clips' / visual-video behavior conflicts with the existing detach-audio workflow. In this repo, detaching audio mutes the original visual clip and creates a new audio-track clip from the same asset, so rendering a waveform on the muted visual clip would duplicate audio structure on a clip that no longer contributes sound.
flags.evidence: [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/editor-utils.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/editor-utils.ts#L188) mutes the source visual clip by setting `volume: 0` and appends a duplicate clip on an audio track; [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx#L87) renders visual-clip audio using `effectiveVolume`, so a muted visual clip is intentionally silent.

flags.concern: timeline-waveform-extraction: the plan makes embedded-video waveforms a must-have but the only extraction path it specifies is `fetch()+decodeAudioData()` on the media URL. The current repo has no existing video-audio extraction helper and the asset model does not record whether a video actually has an audio stream, so this leaves the video-clip half of the feature dependent on a fragile assumption with no repo-backed fallback.
flags.evidence: [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts#L148) shows asset metadata only tracks `file/type/duration/resolution/fps` and not `hasAudio`; [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/package.json](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/package.json#L44) includes Remotion media/player/web-renderer but not `@remotion/media-utils`, so the plan is not reusing an existing extraction path in this codebase.
